design_decisions.switch_primitive: REUSE M2 arch::ctx_switch UNCHANGED, invoked through the existing safe yield_to (no new full-frame primitive). Proof of sufficiency: the per-arch IRQ entry already saved the FULL interrupted frame on the running task's OWN kernel stack BEFORE any Rust ran (x86_64 __alltraps 176-byte TrapFrame; vector 0x20 has IST=0 so the CPU builds it on the current ring0 stack. aarch64 __vec_irq SAVE_CONTEXT 0x110-byte frame; the EL1h IRQ keeps SP_EL1 so it sits on the current stack). Call chain at schedule(): [full IRQ frame] -> trap_handler -> try_handle_irq/handle_irq -> dispatch_irq -> schedule -> yield_to -> ctx_switch. ctx_switch swaps only the callee-saved continuation + SP; the next task resumes by unwinding ITS suspended handler chain back to the IRQ epilogue's iretq/eret, which restores ITS full frame at ITS interrupted instruction. CORRECTION APPLIED (review-required): the aarch64 ctx_switch Safety doc previously said 'Cooperative single-core use only -- never call from a trap handler', which M9 actively violates; that false clause is deleted and replaced with the EL1h/SP_EL1 justification, and the x86_64 ctx_switch doc is harmonized with the identical (IST=0 / RFLAGS.IF) justification. lib.rs yield_to's SAFETY comment is tightened: the 'no other context can touch either slot mid-switch' guarantee now rests on RFLAGS.IF / PSTATE.I being masked from exception entry until the switched-in task's own iretq/eret, not on cooperativeness.
design_decisions.runqueue: In crates/tb-hal/src/lib.rs so the kernel crate stays unsafe-free: static RUNQUEUE:[AtomicUsize;MAX_TASKS] = [const { AtomicUsize::new(usize::MAX) }; MAX_TASKS] (usize::MAX = empty; same inline-const init pattern TASK_SP already uses, so it compiles on the pinned nightly), static RUNQUEUE_LEN:AtomicUsize, static INVOLUNTARY_SWITCHES:AtomicU64. scheduler_init() seeds RUNQUEUE[0] = CURRENT_TASK (the bootstrap context), LEN=1. scheduler_spawn(stack,entry)->Task = M2 task_create() UNCHANGED + enqueue. schedule(_irq_id:u64) is a pub fn with the exact fn(u64) shape set_irq_hook needs (param underscored to avoid an unused warning): no-op if LEN<2, else round-robins to RUNQUEUE[(i+1)%LEN] of the current slot, bumps INVOLUNTARY_SWITCHES on a real switch, and calls yield_to(Task(next)). involuntary_switch_count()->u64 exposes the counter. Round-robin only; QoS lane deferred. Every new pub item is documented (tb-hal is #![deny(missing_docs)]).
design_decisions.marker_task_regains_control: The marker-printing boot task (rust_main, slot 0) is admitted as RUNQUEUE[0] via scheduler_init(), ALONGSIDE spin tasks C and D, so round-robin order is boot->C->D->boot. Every rotation a timer tick involuntarily switches back to the boot task, which resumes THROUGH its own IRQ epilogue inside its wait loop, observes involuntary_switch_count() >= REQUIRED_SWITCHES (100), then calls timer_disarm() (mask + stop) and prints 'M9: preempt OK'. Deadlock-free: the spin tasks never disable interrupts, so the periodic timer keeps firing and the rotation always progresses. FAIL-CLOSED HARDENED (review-required): the old raw SPIN_CAP=5e9 spin guard could out-run the 15 s QEMU cap under TCG before tripping; it is replaced by a tick_count() STAGNATION guard (STALL_LIMIT=100_000_000). last_ticks/stalled reset whenever tick_count() advances, so on a healthy boot stalled never accumulates across a slice (max spins-in-one-~1ms-slice is far below STALL_LIMIT, no false trip); only a genuinely dead timer (tick_count never advances) lets stalled reach STALL_LIMIT and break early into a clean 'M9: FAIL' well inside every runner window.
design_decisions.eoi_ordering: REQUIRED reorder, already present on BOTH arches and confirmed on disk; its stale doc descriptions were also corrected. x86_64 try_handle_irq: lapic_write(LAPIC_EOI,0) is emitted BEFORE crate::dispatch_irq(vector) (else the in-service ISR bit stays set when schedule() ctx_switches away, blocking every further timer IRQ and starving the switched-in task; IF=0 from the interrupt gate prevents any nested tick between the EOI and the switch until the switched-in task's iretq). aarch64 handle_irq: write_cntp_tval(period) de-asserts the level-sensitive PPI (clears CNTP_CTL.ISTATUS) FIRST, then gicc_write(GICC_EOIR,iar) BEFORE crate::dispatch_irq (drops the GIC running priority so the CPU interface forwards the next tick to the switched-in task); the spurious-INTID (>=1020) path still takes NO EOI per GICv2 IHI 0048; any other forwarded INTID still gets its EOI in the else arm; PSTATE.I masked from exception entry until the switched-in task's eret prevents nesting. The handler doc-comments (which previously read 'dispatch_irq, then EOI') were corrected to 'EOI FIRST, then dispatch_irq' so the documentation matches the code on M9's headline change.
design_decisions.timer_rearm: M8's timer_demo left the timer MASKED after its canary, so M9 added safe per-arch exports (in timer.rs, re-exported through {x86_64,aarch64}/mod.rs -> arch/mod.rs -> lib.rs): tb_hal::timer_rearm() (x86_64 = lapic_enable()+lapic_timer_arm()+irq_enable(sti); aarch64 = gic_init()+timer_start()+irq_unmask(daifclr) -- both idempotent, the M8 device page/controller persists so no re-map), tb_hal::timer_disarm() (x86_64 = cli + lapic_timer_disarm; aarch64 = daifset + timer_stop), and tb_hal::irq_unmask() (x86_64 sti / aarch64 daifclr only, no timer touch). The hook is set BEFORE arming (decision 5): the kernel does scheduler_init() -> set_irq_hook(tb_hal::schedule) -> scheduler_spawn(C) -> scheduler_spawn(D) -> timer_rearm(). All controller pokes + the first sti/daifclr live in arch::*::timer; the kernel only calls the safe facade.
design_decisions.spin_tasks: Two never-yielding kernel tasks spin_c/spin_d on fresh 32 KiB static stacks (STACK_C/STACK_D: TaskStack<4096>). Each calls tb_hal::irq_unmask() EXACTLY ONCE at the top -- a task first-activated through ctx_switch's plain ret/br is entered with RFLAGS.IF clear (x86) / PSTATE.I set (aarch64) because it never passed through an iretq/eret that would restore them, so it must self-unmask to become preemptible -- then loops forever incrementing its OWN AtomicU64 (SPIN_C_COUNT/SPIN_D_COUNT) with core::hint::spin_loop(); NO yield_to, NO wfi/hlt, NO cli, so the periodic timer is their ONLY way to lose the CPU (exactly the involuntary-preemption property M9 proves). The self-test asserts BOTH counters advanced (c_ran>0 && d_ran>0) AND involuntary_switch_count() >= 100. They never return; M2's task_exit_guard backstops the impossible case loudly. No per-task TSS.rsp0 / SP_EL1 juggling is needed: every task runs at ring0/EL1 on its own kernel stack, so the CPU builds each IRQ frame on the current stack automatically (a comment flags the user-frame variant for M12).

patches.old_string:
///  * `TIMER_VECTOR` (0x20): bump the monotonic tick (+ run the M9 hook) via the
///    arch-neutral `crate::dispatch_irq`, then EOI the LAPIC.

selftest: Every boot runs the cumulative M0..M9 self-test. After 'M7: heap OK', the M8 block prints 'M8: timer OK' then the in-guest 'boot-cycles=0x..' line. The M9 block then slots in (replacing the final tb_hal::halt(), which still runs last) and prints 'preempt-test: arming round-robin preemption', then: (1) scheduler_init() enqueues the boot task (slot 0); (2) set_irq_hook(tb_hal::schedule) points the M8 tick at the round-robin scheduler BEFORE arming; (3) scheduler_spawn(STACK_C, spin_c) + scheduler_spawn(STACK_D, spin_d) enqueue two no-yield spin tasks (round-robin boot->C->D->boot); (4) timer_rearm() re-arms the M8-masked periodic timer and unmasks interrupts. The boot task then spins until involuntary_switch_count() >= 100 (REQUIRED_SWITCHES), fail-closed on tick_count() STAGNATION (STALL_LIMIT) rather than a raw spin cap, so a dead timer yields a clean 'M9: FAIL' inside the runner window instead of hanging. Each tick involuntarily switches the running task via the REUSED M2 ctx_switch; the boot task loses the CPU to C/D and later resumes through its own IRQ epilogue at the loop instruction, observing the count climb. After >=100 switches it calls timer_disarm() (mask + stop) so the verdict renders with no further preemption, prints the diagnostic 'preempt: involuntary switches=0x..', asserts switches>=100 AND SPIN_C_COUNT>0 AND SPIN_D_COUNT>0 (each nonzero only if that no-yield task was involuntarily scheduled), then prints EXACTLY 'M9: preempt OK\\n' and halts. Regression guard: M2's cooperative ping-pong (same ctx_switch primitive) runs UNCHANGED earlier in the same boot and still prints 'M2: context-switch OK'. Gating: scripts/run-x86_64.sh (QEMU microvm, 15 s), scripts/run-aarch64.sh (QEMU virt, 15 s), scripts/run-vmm-x86_64.sh (tb-vmm, 30 s) grep MARKER='M9: preempt OK'; scripts/bench-boot.sh FINAL_MARKER='M9: preempt OK'. ~100 ticks at the M8 ~1 ms period land in ~100 ms, far inside every cap. Build arbiter: cargo kbuild --target targets/x86_64-tabos-none.json AND targets/aarch64-tabos-none.json, both 0 warnings.
build_notes: CRITICAL CONTEXT (verified this session): the working tree was ALREADY converged to an equivalent M9 state before I started -- HEAD is the M8 commit 916169e but `git diff` shows uncommitted M9 edits across lib.rs (full scheduler facade at lines 729-872), both timer.rs (EOI-before-dispatch reorder + timer_rearm/timer_disarm/sched_irq_unmask), the three mod.rs re-exports, kernel/src/main.rs (M9 block + spin_c/spin_d + AtomicU64), all four scripts (MARKER/FINAL_MARKER + headers advanced to M9), docs/BENCHMARKS.md line 45, and docs/ROADMAP-V2.md §6 line 344 (M9 already marked complete, M10 in progress). I READ every real file; the candidates' M8-anchored old_strings DO NOT match the current disk (e.g. x86 try_handle_irq is already EOI-first), so I did NOT re-apply them and did NOT emit redundant patches for content already on disk. Therefore the FINAL apply-ready edit-set in modified_files is exactly the 7 review-required DELTA patches (5 files) against the CURRENT converged tree; I verified each old_string is byte-exact by APPLYING it (every Edit returned replaced:true / occurrences:1), so all seven apply cleanly. Deltas: (1) aarch64 sched.rs -- deleted the now-FALSE ctx_switch Safety clause 'Cooperative single-core use only -- never call from a trap handler' (M9 deliberately calls it from EL1h IRQ context) and replaced it with the SP_EL1/SAVE_CONTEXT justification [review-1 required_fix]; (2) x86_64 sched.rs -- harmonized ctx_switch with the identical IST=0/RFLAGS.IF justification (the two arches previously disagreed) [review-1]; (3) lib.rs -- tightened yield_to's SAFETY comment so the no-aliasing guarantee rests on IF/PSTATE.I masking across the switch, not cooperativeness [review-1]; (4+5) kernel/src/main.rs -- replaced the raw SPIN_CAP=5e9 fail-closed (which under TCG could out-run the 15 s cap before tripping) with a tick_count() STAGNATION guard (STALL_LIMIT=100_000_000) so a dead timer fails clean inside the window while a healthy boot never false-trips [review-2 required_fix]; (6+7) both timer.rs -- corrected the handler doc-comments that still described the OLD 'dispatch then EOI' order, the exact docs-match-code defect class the ctx_switch fix targets. COMPILE GOTCHAS (CI must confirm; see below): (a) RUNQUEUE uses inline-const array init `[const { AtomicUsize::new(usize::MAX) }; MAX_TASKS]` -- same construct TASK_SP already uses, so it compiles under the pinned nightly. (b) schedule's parameter is `_irq_id` (leading underscore) to keep the exact `fn(u64)` shape set_irq_hook requires without an unused-variable warning. (c) tb-hal is #![deny(missing_docs)] -- every new pub item is documented (scheduler_init/scheduler_spawn/schedule/involuntary_switch_count/timer_rearm/timer_disarm/irq_unmask + per-arch timer_rearm/timer_disarm/sched_irq_unmask). (d) Kernel stays unsafe-free: grep confirms the only `unsafe` token is `#[unsafe(no_mangle)]` on rust_main (plus the panic handler); the new wait loop uses tb_hal::tick_count(), already a used export (kernel line 698 in the M8 block) and AtomicU64 is already imported, so no new import. (e) The new loop's `last_ticks`/`stalled` are both read and reassigned, so no unused/unused-mut warnings; no stray references to the removed `guard`/`SPIN_CAP` remain (grep verified). (f) Fixes 1-3,6-7 are doc-comment-only and cannot affect codegen or warnings. ARBITER NOT RUN HERE (environmental, matches all three reviews): this Windows host has ONLY stable rustc/cargo 1.92.0 -- no rustup, no nightly, no rust-src -- and `cargo kbuild` needs -Zbuild-std + -Zjson-target-spec (nightly), so the custom-target build and QEMU/tb-vmm boot could NOT execute. Before merge, CI (pinned nightly + rust-src + llvm-tools per rust-toolchain.toml) MUST run `cargo kbuild --target targets/x86_64-tabos-none.json` AND `--target targets/aarch64-tabos-none.json` at 0 warnings on BOTH, then boot to 'M9: preempt OK' under QEMU x86_64 microvm + QEMU aarch64 virt + tb-vmm (/dev/kvm), and bench-boot.sh, all also still observing 'M2: context-switch OK'. Source review found no warning trigger or boot-breaking defect; this is the confirmation gate, not a code change.
